description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular surface and a variety of textures. Its coloration is predominantly light gray with patches of tan and brown, and it features numerous small pits, crevices, and weathered indentations. The rock appears to be composed of a dense, solid stone, possibly granite or a similar igneous material, with some darker mineral inclusions visible on certain faces. One side of the rock is more rounded, while the opposite side is flatter and slightly concave, suggesting it may have been partially embedded in soil or exposed to weathering. The surface is matte and rough, with no visible polish or artificial treatment. This rock would be a typical feature in an urban park, roadside landscaping, or as a natural obstacle in a public space.
description: A rugged, light gray rock with tan and brown patches, featuring rough, pitted surfaces and angular edges. Its irregular shape and weathered texture make it suitable for urban landscaping or as a natural barrier.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's most angular and rugged face, with prominent ridges and deep crevices.
description_view_1: This side reveals a flatter, slightly concave surface with visible dark mineral spots and a more weathered appearance.
description_view_2: From this angle, the rock appears elongated and thin, highlighting its uneven, jagged edge and rough texture.
description_view_3: This view displays the broadest, flattest side of the rock, with a relatively smooth but still rough surface and subtle color variations.

license_info.creator_display_name: UQ School of Earth and Environmental Sciences
